mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 3-month annualized growth rate of RSAFS is positive, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over the last 3 months
failure_condition: If, when UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is high and the Federal Reserve is actively raising interest rates, it signals a restrictive monetary policy that increases the cost of capital and reduces future earnings valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 0.25 percentage points in the last month
failure_condition: If, under the specified trigger conditions, the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth tends to moderate but remains positive, indicating a stable, mature economic phase that supports continued, albeit slower, corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 6-month annualized growth rate of INDPRO is between 0% and 3% (inclusive) and the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate robust demand in the housing sector, which has significant spillover effects on related industries and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If, when HOUST 3-month average growth exceeds 5%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, supporting consumer income and spending, which in turn drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the 3-month average change in PAYEMS is positive, but the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses a significant threshold, it signals a more restrictive monetary policy stance by the central bank, which typically dampens economic activity and equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If, after FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust GDP growth and strong retail sales indicates broad-based economic expansion driven by both production and consumption, creating a favorable environment for corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the annualized quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month average growth is > 0.5%, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.
